62df8012e66cc66ca571cfe5.leadin: He fictionalized the battle between the ancients and the moderns in his satire "The Battle of the Books." For 10 points each:
62df8012e66cc66ca571cfe5.leadin_sanitized: He fictionalized the battle between the ancients and the moderns in his satire "The Battle of the Books." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Irish author who described Laputa and Brobdingnag in Gulliver’s Travels.
On one of his travels, Gulliver meets this race of horse-like creatures whose nobility contrasts with the dirtiness of the Yahoos.
Swift adopted this pseudonym to predict the demise of the Almanac editor John Partridge. This pseudonym was named as the head editor of The Tatler by Richard Steele.
